segments.text: I am the man who has seen affliction by the rod of His wrath.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: He has led me and made me walk In darkness and not in light.
Surely He has turned His hand against me Time and time again throughout the day.
He has aged my flesh and my skin, And broken my bones.
He has besieged me And surrounded me with bitterness and woe.
He has set me in dark places Like the dead of long ago.
He has hedged me in so that I cannot get out; He has made my chain heavy.
Even when I cry and shout, He shuts out my prayer.
He has blocked my ways with hewn stone; He has made my paths crooked.
He has been to me a bear lying in wait, Like a lion in ambush.
He has turned aside my ways and torn me in pieces; He has made me desolate.
He has bent His bow And set me up as a target for the arrow.
He has caused the arrows of His quiver To pierce my loins.
I have become the ridicule of all my people— Their taunting song all the day.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: He has filled me with bitterness, He has made me drink wormwood.
He has also broken my teeth with gravel, And covered me with ashes.
You have moved my soul far from peace; I have forgotten prosperity.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: My strength and my hope Have perished from the Lord.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Remember my affliction and roaming, The wormwood and the gall.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: My soul still remembers And sinks within me.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: This I recall to my mind, Therefore I have hope.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Through the Lord's mercies we are not consumed, Because His compassions fail not.
They are new every morning; Great is Your faithfulness.
The Lord is my portion, Therefore I hope in Him!
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: The Lord is good to those who wait for Him, To the soul who seeks Him.
It is good that one should hope and wait quietly For the salvation of the Lord.
It is good for a man to bear The yoke in his youth.
Let him sit alone and keep silent, Because God has laid it on him;
Let him put his mouth in the dust— There may yet be hope.
Let him give his cheek to the one who strikes him, And be full of reproach.
For the Lord will not cast off forever.
Though He causes grief, Yet He will show compassion According to the multitude of His mercies.
For He does not afflict willingly, Nor grieve the children of men.
To crush under one's feet All the prisoners of the earth,
To turn aside the justice due a man Before the face of the Most High,
Or subvert a man in his cause— The Lord does not approve.
Who is he who speaks and it comes to pass, When the Lord has not commanded it?
Is it not from the mouth of the Most High That woe and well-being proceed?
Why should a living man complain, A man for the punishment of his sins?
Let us search out and examine our ways, And turn back to the Lord;
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Let us lift our hearts and hands To God in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: We have transgressed and rebelled; You have not pardoned.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: You have covered Yourself with anger And pursued us; You have slain and not pitied.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: You have covered Yourself with a cloud, That prayer should not pass through.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: You have made us an offscouring and refuse In the midst of the peoples.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: All our enemies Have opened their mouths against us.
Fear and a snare have come upon us, Desolation and destruction.
My eyes overflow with rivers of water For the destruction of the daughter of my people.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: My eyes flow and do not cease, Without interruption,
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Till the Lord from heaven Looks down and sees.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: My eyes bring suffering to my soul Because of all the daughters of my city.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: My enemies without cause Hunted me down like a bird.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: They silenced my life in the pit And threw stones at me.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: The waters flowed over my head; I said, I am cut off!
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: I called on Your name, O Lord, From the lowest pit.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: You have heard my voice: Do not hide Your ear From my sighing, from my cry for help.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Do not fear!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: O Lord, You have pleaded the case for my soul; You have redeemed my life.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: O Lord, You have seen how I am wronged; Judge my case.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: You have seen all their vengeance, All their schemes against me.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: You have heard their reproach, O Lord, All their schemes against me,
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: The lips of my enemies And their whispering against me all the day.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Look at their sitting down and their rising up; I am their taunting song.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Repay them, O Lord, According to the work of their hands.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Give them a veiled heart; Your curse be upon them!
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: In Your anger, Pursue and destroy them From under the heavens of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah
